data.title: Gold and silver slide on renewed Middle East war tensions
data.description:
Gold and silver futures fell Monday as ​the dollar and oil ​prices rose following Iran's threat of retaliation in response to the seizure of an Iranian cargo vessel by U.S. forces, dimming hopes of a breakthrough in efforts to end the Middle East war.

Iran wavered on whether to send diplomats to Pakistan for a second round of peace talks after the U.S. seized the ship and maintained its blockade of the Strait of Hormuz.

The U.S. dollar rose to its highest level in a week before paring gains to trade up ‌0.1%, and benchmark 10-year U.S. Treasury yields gained, increasing ​the opportunity cost of holding non-yielding bullion.

"The latest weakness has been driven by renewed dollar strength and fresh concerns about energy-led inflation," Saxo Bank analysts said in a note. "Gold and silver remain highly sensitive to developments in the Middle East given the knock-on impact on the dollar, bond yields, and U.S. rate expectations."

Meanwhile, gold demand during one of India's key buying festivals remained subdued on Sunday as record prices ​curbed jewelry purchases, offsetting a ‌modest uptick in investment demand.

Front-month Comex gold (XAUUSD:CUR) for April delivery closed 1% lower at $4,806.60/oz, and front-month Comex April silver (XAGUSD:CUR) settled down 2.2% to $79.951/oz.

ETFs: (GLD), (GDX), (GDXJ), (IAU), (NUGT), (PHYS), (GLDM), (AAAU), (SGOL), (DUST), (RING), (BAR), (OUNZ), (SLV), (PSLV), (SIVR), (SIL), (SILJ)

data.title: Snap appoints insider Doug Hott as new CFO
data.description:
Snap (SNAP) said it has appointed insider Doug Hott as its new Chief Financial Officer.

The social media company’s current CFO, Derek Andersen, will depart, effective May 8, 2026, according to an SEC filing.

Hott, 53, is the current Vice President of Finance, Strategy, and Corporate Development in the company since July 2024. Before that, Hott served as Snap’s Vice President of Finance.

The company's stock was trading 0.8% lower after-hours on Monday.

data.title: RTX's unit wins $213M contract modification for Zumwalt systems upgrade
data.description:
• Raytheon Missiles and Defense, a unit of RTX (RTX), won a $213.4M contract modification on Monday for Zumwalt-class Navy combat systems.
  • The contract covers installation, integration, testing, maintenance, and upgrades of mission systems.
  • The work is expected to be completed by April 2027.
  • The FY25 and FY26 Navy budget funds will be obligated at the time of award, of which ~$32.3M will expire by the end of FY26.
  • The contract is managed by Naval Sea Systems Command.
  • The stock traded slightly higher at $195.96 in the after-hours trading session.

data.title: Westlake Chemical Partners LP names new CFO
data.description:
• Westlake Chemical Partners (WKL) appointed Jonathan H. Baksht as senior vice president, chief financial officer, and director effective June 15, 2026.
  • Baksht will succeed M. Steven Bender, who will retire and transition to special advisor.
  • Bender will also step down from the board of the general partner on the same date.
  • Baksht most recently served as CFO of Fortune Brands Innovations.

data.title: Alaska Air GAAP EPS of -$1.69 beats by $0.01, revenue of $3.3B beats by $10M
data.description:
• Alaska Air press release (ALK): Q1 GAAP EPS of -$1.69 beats by $0.01.
  • Revenue of $3.3B (+5.1% Y/Y) beats by $10M.
  • Outlook:
  • For full year 2026, our visibility to earnings is limited due primarily to ongoing fuel price volatility. Until conditions stabilize and we have better sight to earnings beyond the current quarter, we have suspended full-year guidance. Similarly, for the second quarter, the range of potential financial outcomes remains wide and difficult to predict, as recent geopolitical factors have resulted in sharp and unpredictable changes in fuel prices. As a result, we're providing detailed assumptions on unit revenue and unit costs, in lieu of our traditional EPS guidance range.
    Second quarter capacity is expected to be up approximately 1% year-over-year, down nearly a point from original expectations, reflecting proactive trimming of capacity in May and June. Second quarter unit revenues are trending to be up high single digits year-over-year, with a path to increasing 10% year-over-year, assuming demand strength and yield trends sustain the rest of the period. This expectation is despite a 2-point unit revenue headwind from storms in Hawai'i that have impacted near term demand.
    Second quarter year-over-year unit cost performance is expected to be approximately 1.5 points higher than the first quarter, driven by close‑in capacity reductions and several transitory factors. These include crew training costs associated with the ramp‑up of international widebody flying, a year‑over‑year headwind from aircraft sale gains in the second quarter of 2025, and current year planned employee recognition expense tied to achieving a single passenger service system - an important integration milestone. Unit costs are expected to inflect downward in the second half of the year to low single‑digit growth.
    Fuel remains the largest source of near‑term uncertainty. April fuel is expected to be approximately $4.75 per gallon, and we expect the quarter to average approximately $4.50 based on the forward curve today. This assumption adds approximately $600 million of expense to the second quarter, equivalent to an earnings per share headwind of $3.60. We expect to consume approximately 297 million gallons of fuel in the quarter based on our current capacity plan.
  • Shares -1.7% AH.

data.title: Netstreit trades lower after mixed quarterly earnings, 2026 guidance raised
data.description:
Netstreit (NTST) was trading lower after the retail REIT reported mixed quarterly financial results.

Shares were 0.91% down to $20.70 during after-market trading on Monday.

Adjusted FFO of $0.34, up 6.3% year-over-year, was in line with the S&P Cap IQ consensus estimate.

Revenue of $57.06M increased from $45.91M a year ago, against the consensus of $55.46M.

As of March 31, the retail REIT had an occupancy of 99.9%.

"I am pleased to report a strong start to the year with a record amount of net investments completed this quarter. Our disciplined sourcing and underwriting allowed us to capitalize on an attractive acquisitions market while maintaining our strict risk-adjusted return targets," said CEO Mark Manheimer.

"Given the excellent condition of our balance sheet, which was strengthened by $314M of gross forward equity sales in the quarter, we are increasing both our 2026 net investment guidance and the midpoint of our 2026 AFFO per share guidance," added Manheimer.

The company raised the lower end of its full-year 2026 adjusted FFO guidance range to $1.36-$1.39 from $1.35-$1.39. The consensus stands at $1.38.

AFFO per share guidance range now includes $0.03-$0.06 per share of estimated dilution (vs. the previous $0.015-$0.03 per share) due to the impact of the company's outstanding forward equity calculated in accordance with the treasury stock method.

Net investment activity guidance was increased to $550.0M-$650.0M from $350.0M-$450.0M.

Earnings conference call is scheduled for Tuesday, April 21, at 11:00 AM ET.

data.title: Stocks to watch on Monday after hours: AAPL, AMZN, STLD
data.description:
Wall Street finished lower on Monday as hopes for a potential U.S.-Iran peace deal dimmed, with rising tensions in the Middle East dampening investor sentiment.

Here are the 3 stocks to watch on Monday after hours:

Apple Inc. (AAPL) shares edged lower by 0.8% after reports that Tim Cook will step down as CEO following nearly two decades at the helm, transitioning into the role of executive chairman. He will be succeeded by John Ternus, marking a significant leadership shift for the tech giant.

Amazon (AMZN) shares climbed 2.5% in after-hours trading as the company announced today it was investing $5B in Anthropic (ANTHRO) and plans to add up to $20B more to that figure in the years ahead.

Steel Dynamics (STLD) shares fell 1% after hours as the company reported first-quarter GAAP EPS of $2.78, missing estimates by $0.02. At the same time, revenue came in at $5.2B, up 19% year-over-year and beating expectations by $140M.
